message:
fix: clamp HTTP buffer consume to bounds and include array length in error

Two small correctness fixes:

1. http/client.rs: Clamp Buffer::consume() position to bytes.len()
   after saturating_add, preventing pos from exceeding the buffer
   length. Without this, a sufficiently large 'n' could leave pos
   pointing past valid data even though it wouldn't panic (due to
   the == check on the next line).

2. extensions_js.rs: Include the actual array length in the error
   message when it exceeds the 100,000 element limit. Changes from
   static "Array length exceeds..." to dynamic "Array length (N)
   exceeds..." for easier debugging of oversized payloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
